untranslatable_reason: This signal is a meta-rule about rule validation and regime bias detection, not a direct entry/exit trading rule. It requires: (1) counting trigger frequencies across entry conditions (not available as indicators), (2) comparing backtest performance across opposite trend regimes programmatically (requires backtesting framework logic, not condition-based rules), and (3) statistical analysis of performance divergence (>35% swing detection). These are backtesting validation checks, not price-action conditions. The rule cannot be expressed as simple indicator conditions.
translation_notes: ARONSON_DRY_0_2 is a validation framework for detecting directional bias in rules, not a tradeable signal itself. It describes how to TEST rules (symmetry of triggers, regime performance comparison) rather than WHEN to trade. To implement this properly requires: (a) rule symmetry analysis code outside the condition engine, (b) regime-split backtest comparison logic, and (c) statistical divergence thresholds on aggregate metrics. Suggest implementing as a pre-backtest rule validator rather than a standard condition-based signal.
